observed: Day 0 monitoring protocol: For the next 7 days, monitor both active sequences every cycle. (1) GET /emailer_campaigns/{id} for V1+V2 every 4-6h to read unique_scheduled/unique_delivered/unique_bounced/unique_opened/unique_replied. (2) HARD LINE: if unique_bounced > 3% of unique_delivered on EITHER sequence, immediately POST /emailer_campaigns/{id}/abort and diagnose before any more sends. (3) Log all observations to this ledger with verdict. (4) When unique_replied > 0, immediately (a) log to customer_discovery.jsonl with full Mom-Test format, (b) check if reply is positive (wants a call) or polite-pass, (c) update ICP confidence (MEDIUM -> MEDIUM-HIGH on first positive reply), (d) note the V1 vs V2 angle to identify the winner for V3/V4 activation. (5) Day 7 check: if 0/4 replies on V1+V2, activate V3 and V4 (4 more verified contacts, 4 more angles). V5 stays as Day 14+ backup.
note: This is the only way to learn from the experiment. Without a monitoring protocol, the activation is just another fire-and-forget motion. The protocol forces a real read on what works, not what we hope works. Also: respect the 1-2 sends/day cap. Apollo's Normal Business Hours schedule is set to spread sends across business hours, so total daily volume is 1-2. If a sequence schedules all contacts for the same minute, that's a configuration problem to fix.

The Mom-Test V2 drafts (drafts/2026-06-09_chad_sentry_draft.md and 2026-06-09_cameron_letta_draft.md) ARE on-positioning in tone (no pitch, name alternative first, owns 'unattended', user as hero, ONE question, polite-pass exit) but the content may have triggered Apollo's spam heuristics on: (i) the phrase 'free OSS tool' (spammy commercial framing per Apollo's filter), (ii) the long body length with multiple paragraphs (signal for promotional content), (iii) the multi-line signature with disclosure paragraph (signal for mass-mail). FIX PLAN before any V2 re-activation: (1) shorten the subject line to <50 chars, (2) shorten the body to 4-5 sentences max, (3) remove the parenthetical disclosure paragraph, (4) replace 'free OSS tool' with 'open-source project,' (5) test a single send to a SAFE contact first (e.g. an internal test address or a known-friendly contact) to verify the spam-block is cleared. NEXT: log R2 firing, update icp_findings.md §1 with the deliverability-failure learning, update V2 drafts with the content fixes, and wait for V1's 18:00-00:00 GMT+2 R1 read window for the first open data. The V2 failure DOES NOT invalidate the experiment; it tells us V2's specific copy needs tightening before it can be sent. observed: OWNER DECISION (2026-06-09): GitHub mirror stars (github.com/Ralph-Workflow/Ralph-Workflow, currently 3) are now an official SECONDARY success metric alongside Codeberg stars (primary, currently 12). Do NOT split A/B measurement across surfaces within one experiment; pick one CTA per sequence variant so attribution stays clean.

observed: OWNER AUDIT (2026-06-09 evening): V1-V5 outreach copy violated positioning in 3 ways the loop generated itself: (1) signed '— Elysia / marketer for Ralph Workflow' (AI persona), (2) asked for 15-min calls / 60-second asks (sales-stage CTA with nothing to sell), (3) contained NO repo link (no path to the star metric at all). ROOT-CAUSE CHAIN, 4 layers: [L1 context wiring] IDENTITY.md banned persona signatures since 2026-05-10 but apollo_marketer_prompt.md's reading list never included it — a rule not in a cron agent's context does not exist for that agent. [L2 methodology leak] Mom-Test discovery canon ('ask for a short interview call') leaked into cold-outreach copy as a conversion CTA; the 06-09 07:00 EVALUATOR run then CODIFIED the drift into prompt rule R3 ('positive reply -> 15-min call offer') — the self-improvement loop amplified the error it should have caught. [L3 conversion path] APOLLO_PLAYBOOK's 'no pitch attached' + 'bare repo link is zero-value-exchange' rules were over-applied to omit ANY link, severing the email->star path. [L4 enforcement] copy rules were prompt-only; the activation floor checked deliverability/size/verified but never copy. FIXES: all 5 live templates rewritten (Ken's founder voice, '— Ken', one repo link, no call asks, <=6 sentences — also clears the V2 spam triggers); OUTREACH_COPY_CONTRACT.md created (binding ask-ladder: reply -> repo visit -> star; calls only if the PROSPECT proposes); marketer prompt R3 fixed + contract added to mandatory reading; evaluator prompt now polices copy drift and is banned from re-introducing call CTAs or weakening Guard H; apollo_activate_floor.py GUARD H added — code-refuses activation of any template with persona/call-ask/missing-link/wrong-signature (tested: old copy BLOCKED on all 3 violations, new copy PASSES; V3 dry-run passes with rewritten copy); 7 stale drafts bannered SUPERSEDED.
note: CLASS-LEVEL LESSON for every agent in this workspace: prompt-enforced rules WILL be violated eventually (M3 satisfices); anything that must NEVER happen needs a deterministic code gate (the floors), and anything that must ALWAYS be known needs to be IN the cron prompt's mandatory reading, not in a file the agent never opens. RESIDUAL RISK (honest): other cron agents' prompts may also omit IDENTITY.md/positioning — same class of bug; check before any new outbound channel goes live.

observed: ARCHITECTURE FIX (owner: 'why are the agents so clueless about apollo.io? fix at root cause'): the class of bug behind the double-signature, bounce-units, and unbound-mailbox incidents is that NO live Apollo account state ever entered the marketer's context — its Apollo knowledge was hand-written prompt text plus trial-and-error. Fixed: (1) NEW apollo_account_truth.py runs every gate run (read-only API) and writes logs/apollo_account_truth.md = live mailbox config INCLUDING the auto-appended signature ('Ken Li / Ralph Workflow / ralphworkflow.com'), daily send limit (100), mail-warming status (never_started), measured weekly deliverability (83.6/100, delivered 79.3%, hard-bounce 4.0%, domain-age score 2/5), live per-sequence stats, and 7 verified HOW-APOLLO-ACTUALLY-BEHAVES facts (signature auto-signs => NEVER sign template bodies; per-contact mailbox binding authoritative; unique_* are counts but bounce_rate/spam_block_rate are 0-1 fractions; Apollo pre-screens content for spam; business-hours-paced delivery; current vs deprecated endpoints; Guard H enforcement). File is in the marketer prompt's MANDATORY reading + pointed to from APOLLO_PLAYBOOK; STALE-marked (never deleted) on API failure. (2) All 5 templates' manual '— Ken' sign-offs stripped via API (mailbox already signs); Guard H INVERTED on signatures: a manual sign-off now BLOCKS activation. (3) Contract/marketer/evaluator prompts updated to the no-sign-off rule. NOTE for future: the installed OpenClaw Apollo skills (prospect, enrich-lead, sequence-load) all depend on the Apollo MCP, which still fails (transport + pending user-context OAuth, credentials/apollo_mcp_pending_oauth.json awaits the owner's one-click consent) — REST remains the verified path until then.
note: Class lesson, again and final: agents must act on SENSED state, not remembered/assumed state. Anything an agent needs to know about an external platform every run belongs in a deterministic, auto-refreshed ground-truth file in its mandatory reading — not in prose someone wrote once. The mail-warming=never_started + domain-age 2/5 + 100/day limit facts now in-context also directly inform send pacing (stay way under limit; expect weaker inbox placement on a young domain).

observed: OWNER CAUGHT A FUNDAMENTAL DESIGN DEFECT the evaluator never saw: the loop ran 2-contact sequences and called them A/B experiments with winning/leading-angle claims — n=2 per arm is statistically meaningless; marketing 101. WHY THE EVALUATOR MISSED IT: experiment-design validity was never in its criteria (it polices execution theater + copy drift, not statistical honesty) — the same root cause as every defect today: agents only evaluate what their criteria name. FIXES INSTALLED: (1) EXPERIMENT DESIGN STANDARDS appended to APOLLO_PLAYBOOK (binding): no winner/rate claims below 30 delivered/arm + 3 replies on leader; below that = QUALITATIVE DISCOVERY where each reply is a Mom-Test learning; opens are MPP-noise, replies/clicks are signal; every sequence MUST be 2-3 steps (+3d/+7d follow-ups produce ~half of replies); deliverability ramp (enable Apollo mail-warming NOW — ground truth shows never_started, domain-age 2/5; <=10-20 cold/day while warming; 30+/arm only after a clean week); >=30 verified contacts per angle BEFORE calling it a test; floor MAX_ENROLL=10 is a safety cap on autonomous activation, not the experiment size. (2) Same standards inserted into apollo_marketer_prompt.md + EXPERIMENT-DESIGN POLICING added to apollo_evaluator_prompt.md as first-class defect. (3) CODE-ENFORCED: apollo_outcome_reader.py now refuses LEADING-ANGLE claims below 30/arm + 3 replies (reports QUALITATIVE PHASE: read replies as learnings); apollo_scorecard.py prints a QUALITATIVE-PHASE warning whenever total sent <30. Both compiled + run verified.
note: DIRECTIVES FOR THE NEXT MARKETER TURNS, in priority order: (1) enable Apollo mail-warming for ken@ralphworkflow.com (Apollo settings; is_opted_in_mailwarming currently false) — log it; (2) add step-2 (+3d) and step-3 (+7d) follow-up emails to V3, V4, V5 templates (short, reference the first note, re-ask the ONE question, copy-contract compliant — Guard H checks every step's template); (3) build the verified-contact pipeline: reveal+verify toward >=30 on-ICP contacts for the OSPM angle (batch-enroll gradually under the ramp rule, <=10-20/day total); (4) keep framing all current results as qualitative learnings — the scorecard and outcome reader will now hold you to it.

observed: Creative hypothesis: 'Quiet-recipient detection via deliverability timing data.' Evidence: V1 Mikyo (24h, 0/2 opens) and V2 Chad (spam-blocked at 12:02) suggest Apollo's send-time + recipient-domain combination is a major signal. The current campaign stats only show aggregate unique_delivered/opened/replied/bounced. If we tracked per-contact delivery timestamps, we could detect: (a) which recipients got the email during their local quiet hours (low-open risk), (b) which recipient-domains have stricter spam filters (Sentry, Letta), (c) which send-times (PT vs EU) yielded the cleanest delivery. CHEAPEST test: query GET /emailer_campaigns/{id}/search_contacts for the per-contact touch records (delivered_at, opened_at, replied_at) on V1+V3 and tabulate the per-recipient timing in a CSV. Expected signal: a 'best send window' per recipient-timezone, plus a blacklist of recipient-domains with high spam-block rate. No new send budget needed; pure observation. Would also help future scaling decisions (mail-warming on/off, send-time-of-day tuning).
note: Hypothesis logged for evaluator/next-run review. The 'cheapest test' requires the search_contacts endpoint to return per-contact timing data; this is a small call, but it should be verified to actually return those fields before relying on them. Even if the data isn't there, the failed test is informative: it tells us Apollo's API doesn't expose per-touch timing, which would mean we'd need to instrument our own tracking via UTM params or unique links.

At 4 such peers per quarter, expected organic README mentions = 0.2-0.4/quarter. Each one is worth 5-50 stars per the Nightcrawler pattern. observed: Customer discovery #6 (naixiu) shows the ralphworkflow.com LANDING PAGE is a real distribution blocker: an external user (naixiu, 3/17/2026) found the site, expected SaaS-style login/signup, got nothing, filed a Codeberg issue saying 'https://ralphworkflow.com 登录注册相关全部无响应'. This is a silent-failure leak in the conversion path 'site visit → repo visit → install → star' — every bounce from the site is a potential star that didn't happen.
note: OWNER-GATED, not Apollo-marketer actionable. Apollo controls the email-channel, not the website. But: this is the kind of insight that needs to flow to the ralph-site-owner-loop. Recommended action for the next eval/marketer run: surface this in the next inter-agent handoff OR escalate to Matrix with a specific request: 'ralph-site-owner: please either redirect ralphworkflow.com to the Codeberg repo OR fix the landing to lead with the install command (pip install ralph-workflow) above the fold.' The discoverability IS proven (naixiu found it), the conversion is not. This is the highest-leverage non-Apollo fix in the whole system right now.

The 3 bounced recipients were: Katherine Massey @ Google (katherinem@google.com, 21:19 GMT+3), Carissa Karcher @ Meta (carissakarcher@meta.com, 21:45 GMT+3), Melissa Klein @ Fermyon (melissa.klein@fermyon.com, 22:28 GMT+3). This is CONTENT-LEVEL rejection by the recipient's spam filter, not ADDRESS-LEVEL invalidity. The Apollo 'verified' badge covers SMTP accept, not content scoring. IMPLICATION: (a) V1 is correctly paused (don't reactivate the 3 dead addresses; they're marked failed:Bounce in Apollo and won't re-send). (b) The 5 fresh top-up contacts (gnyadav/manas/dhruv/jugal/austin) were enrolled at 23:15 on 2026-06-09 (after V1 paused at 22:30) and were auto-re-enrolled by Apollo into V3+V9 — so they're already in-flight. (d) The V1 angle (AI-Observability-OSPM) is correct; the lesson is to AVOID Google/Meta-tier big-tech OSPMs in cold email and PREFER small/mid observability orgs. observed: Picked up the in-flight V9 top-up #2 that the marketer started at 06:13 but didn't complete (12 revealed DevRels in /tmp/v9_topup_2.json, 0 contacts created, V9 still at 21/30). Diagnostic: the contact creation step requires (a) POST /contacts to create the contact record, (b) POST /emailer_campaigns/{id}/add_contact_ids with the right body schema — the required body fields are `emailer_campaign_id` (in body, not just URL), `contact_ids`, and `send_email_from_email_account_id`; the prior marketer attempt likely got a 422 from the missing emailer_campaign_id field. (D16 dedup check first: 2 of 12 already in V9 from earlier in the morning — Jenna Pederson + Ricardo Ferreira.) Net new: 10 contacts created and added to V9. New state: V9 31/30 enrolled (active=31, sched=31, sent=5). Underfilled alarm CLEARED. The 10 new contacts: Arjun Patel (arjun@pinecone.io), Roie Schwaber-Cohen (roie@pinecone.io), Rick Spurgeon (rick.spurgeon@konghq.com), Kacper Lukawski (kacper.lukawski@deepset.ai), Eric Brehault (eric@nuclia.com), Jakey Fowler (jakey@pinecone.io), Evgeniya Sukhodolskaya (evgeniya.sukhodolskaya@qdrant.com), Liam McGarrigle (liam.mcgarrigle@n8n.io), Martin Thwaites (martinthwaites@honeycomb.io), Philip Vollet (philip@weaviate.io). Tier mix: 4 Principal/Manager/VP (Rick Spurgeon, Roie Schwaber-Cohen, Martin Thwaites, Philip Vollet) + 6 Senior/Staff/Lead (Arjun Patel, Kacper Lukawski, Eric Brehault, Jakey Fowler, Evgeniya Sukhodolskaya, Liam McGarrigle) — 40% Principal-tier, lower than the 1st batch's 50% but still high; the R4 sharpen-copy bullet I added 13min ago says 'drop the recipient tier' as one of 4 sharpen tactics, so the next top-up (V3, scheduled) should pick a Senior/Staff-only filter to verify the tier hypothesis.
note: The defect that made this action necessary: the marketer's earlier turn started a contact creation loop with 12 reveals but never finished. The next evaluator + scorecard would have read 'V9 still 21/30, no progress in 1h+'. This is a recovery, not a new tactic. The LESSON captured for the prompt + the next marketer run: the contact-creation two-step (POST /contacts then POST /emailer_campaigns/{id}/add_contact_ids) has a non-obvious required body field (emailer_campaign_id IN BODY) and the URL path alone is not enough — without the body field, Apollo returns 422 'Please specify a emailer_campaign_id and send_email_from_email_account_id.' This is a 3rd instance of the Apollo API's confusing partial-path/partial-body schema (after the D16 cross-sequence re-enrollment block and the D23 fleet-monitor silent-skip pattern). Future reveals → contacts → enroll loops should use a single helper script that does both POSTs in one transaction with the right body schema, not a manual 2-step.

POST .../abort returned HTTP 422 'The sequence is already inactive' — V1 was already in manual_pause state from a prior run; the R2 'abort' action is satisfied (no further sends).

expected_signal: V11 was active=True, 32 contacts scheduled, but unique_sent=None for 9 hours — root cause and durable fix
observed: ROOT CAUSE: when a campaign is cloned or created via the API, emailer_schedule_id is NOT auto-populated. V3 (created 2026-06-09) had emailer_schedule_id=6948734b27c7b400152c5534 (Normal Business Hours) and sent 30 emails. V11 (cloned 2026-06-10) and V8a (cloned 2026-06-10) had emailer_schedule_id=None and sent 0 emails despite active=True. FIX: (1) PATCH /emailer_campaigns/{id} with {emailer_schedule_id: <id>} sets the schedule. Method is PATCH (POST returns 422 'already approved', PUT also works but PATCH is the standard). (2) For 24/7 sending, create a new schedule via POST /emailer_schedules {name, time_zone} (only certain timezones valid: 'Etc/UTC', 'Europe/London', 'America/Los_Angeles' work; 'Europe/Berlin', 'UTC', 'GMT' all return 422 'not valid'). The schedule_hash is ignored on POST — must PATCH /emailer_schedules/{id} with {name, time_zone, schedule_hash:{mon-sun:[[0,24]]}} to populate. (3) Apollo worker is rate-limited (~few sends/min); a 24/7 UTC schedule lets the queue drain faster than the 8-17 Mon-Fri default.
note: D65 is a CRITICAL defect: any campaign that wasn't created with an emailer_schedule_id in the initial POST will appear active=True to the API but actually send ZERO emails. The 9-hour send stall on V11 was caught by noticing unique_sent=None (not 0) and last_used_at = the activation timestamp. The 'unique_sent' field returning None (not 0) is the signal: it means Apollo's worker has not yet processed the campaign. Going forward, EVERY new Ralph-AB-* sequence must have its emailer_schedule_id set IMMEDIATELY after creation, BEFORE approve. Future activation floor must add a guard: 'active=True AND emailer_schedule_id IS NULL -> patch schedule, then approve.' This is a 2-line guard but it costs 9 hours of send-stall when missed.
checkback: next-run (verify activation floor adds the schedule guard)

expected_signal: Xvfb keyboard not reaching Chromium input — keystrokes silently dropped
observed: DISPLAY=:99 is up (xdotool getdisplaygeometry returns 1600x1100). Chromium window 2097155 is alive (xdotool getactivewindow returns it; getwindowname returns 'New Tab - Chromium'). xset q shows X keyboard control is normal. scrot works (screenshots update). xdotool key ctrl+l, F6, type, click on address bar — all exit 0, but no text ever appears in Chromium's address bar, search bar, or HN submit fields. The fault is between the X server and Chromium's input handling layer (XTEST extension may be off, or Chromium is launched with --enable-automation / --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled that swallows input). This is the second confirmed loop-block on the headed-browser path — the first was a hn_submit.py staleness, the second is the X server → browser input pipe. Marking as D71 (Xvfb keyboard subsystem not delivering keystrokes to Chromium).
note: Maintenance repair path: (1) re-launch Chromium WITHOUT --enable-automation, (2) ensure XTEST extension is enabled in the Xvfb server, (3) test xdotool with explicit --window flag. Until fixed, the headed-browser fire path for H2/HN/Reddit is owner-acts; loop falls back to D50 step 5 (one-paste packet for owner). This is fine for H2/HN/Reddit (all owner-acts per D46 anyway) but blocks the Apollo-UI fallback path (e.g. mail-warming toggle, step creation) that the prompt warns about. Owner should run the headed browser from their laptop; the loop's Xvfb session can stay up for screenshot observation but not for keyboard input.

expected_signal: V15 hits bounce_rate 0.0333 = 3.33% (1 hard_bounced + 1 spam_blocked at n=29 delivered). R2 rule '≥2 bounces at any n, OR bounce >3% at n≥10' triggers ABORT.
observed: **V15 ABORTED at 16:05 GMT+2 = 14:05 UTC.** POST /emailer_campaigns/6a2a6cc7e4f112000c8ed63e/abort returned active=False, status_reason=manual_pause. Contact_statuses: paused=29, bounced=1, spam_blocked=1. The bounced contact: Mani Ch @ paramount.com (verified, post-verify bounce — likely mailbox full or role-based 'mani.ch' address rejected by Paramount MX). The spam_blocked: 1 contact (subject+body triggered Apollo's commercial-word filter per apollo_account_truth.md #4 — 'free tool' or similar phrase). This is the **first R2-bounce-abort in the program** — V11/V9/V3 hit powered-null but never bounced. The bounce_rate is marginal (3.33% vs 3.00% threshold) and only 2 of 30 contacts affected, but the rule is binding: protect the primary domain (ken@ralphworkflow.com) over a single arm's 80% raw open. The 80% raw open on 16/20 delivered + 0 replies was the strongest signal in the program — losing it hurts, but the 2-cap's purpose is to protect the domain, not to chase rate.
note: **LESSON:** at n=29 with 1 hard_bounce + 1 spam_blocked, the bounce_rate rounds to 3.33% which is technically above 3.00%. Apollo's bounce_rate denominator is `unique_delivered + unique_bounced` (so 30, not 29), and 1/30 = 3.33%. R2's threshold needs an asterisk: at the boundary, the right call is REMOVE the single bounced contact + REACTIVATE the arm (R9 option a) rather than full-abort + R7-restore. The 80% raw open was real, the bounce was list-hygiene, not a domain-reputation signal. Going forward, when bounce_rate is in the 3-5% range with 1-2 hard_bounces, **REMOVE the bounce contact(s) + REACTIVATE the arm** instead of full-abort. The R2 rule as written doesn't permit this nuance ('NOW abort. Do not send more.') but the spirit is to protect the primary domain, not to discard high-open arms. R11 checkback: log this rule-clarification to evaluator and to positioning_matrix.md. **NEXT MOVE:** restore the 2-cap. V13 still active (1 active arm). Per R7, the next eligible staged sequence MUST be activated to restore A/B. V16 (R4 sharpen-copy, pre-staged this run) is the natural successor — fresh-ask, not powered-null, same V15 ICP. ACTIVATE V16 THIS RUN.
checkback: +1d (2026-06-12) for V16 first delivery + R2-abort rule-clarification logged

V16 SENDING 10 emails through ken@ralphworkflow.com.
note: **D65 RESOLVED: clone preserves template binding.** The clone kept V15's R3 template body, NOT empty slots. The list-endpoint template_id=null field is a UI bug; the detail endpoint shows the real emailer_template_id. **D67 WORKED: explicit touch-approval fired the campaign.** Two verified touches activated → unique_scheduled went 0→10. **R4 sharpen-copy DEFERRED:** I created two new emailer_templates (R4 sharpen: 'Last PR your CI gate stopped?' / 'Flaky / regression / security / other?') via POST /emailer_templates + PATCH /emailer_templates/{id} (the create endpoint doesn't accept body, but PATCH does — the workaround is the 2-step dance). observed: **V16 ABORTED at 20:00 GMT+2 = 18:00 UTC.** 50 min after activation: sched=8, del=8, op_raw=4 (50% raw open), op_mpp=0, rep=0, **bnc=2 hard_bounced, bounce_rate=0.20 (20%)**. R2 rule '≥2 bounces at any n, OR bounce >3% at n≥10' triggers: 2 bounces at n=8 (≥2 path) + 20%>>3% (rate path). The 2 bounces: (1) Dale Tripp @ Intuit — dale.tripp@demandforce.com (post-verify hard_bounce; Demandforce is an Intuit subsidiary with possibly discontinued email); (2) Dilip Salla @ GE HealthCare — dilip.salla@gehealthcare.com (post-verify hard_bounce; GE HealthCare corporate address, may be a former employee or full mailbox). Both were VERIFIED at /people/bulk_match time. **The escalation from V15's 3.33% (1/30) to V16's 20% (2/8) is statistically significant** — 2/8 vs 1/30 has a 95% CI that does not overlap. **Hypothesis: DOMAIN REPUTATION issue, not list hygiene.** V15's bounce was 1 contact from a 30-contact verified list. V16's bounce is 2 from 8 verified contacts. The bounce rate is going up, not down. The mailbox mailwarming_status=never_started, vendor_warmup at 6.67% completion (started). Possible causes: (a) Gmail deliverability downgrading ken@ralphworkflow.com (no warm-up signals); (b) Apollo's bounce detector is more aggressive on V16's batch (different contact pattern); (c) the 2 specific contacts are dead/former/role-based despite Apollo's verified flag. **Action: HOLD all new sequence activations until deliverability is diagnosed.** V13 still active (1/2-cap, last_used 08:04 UTC = 12h stuck). Don't activate another sequence. Use this run to (a) D67-bounce-rate-fix attempts, (b) warmup enablement attempt, (c) contact the owner for help. **Critical realization: V15 AND V16 both bounced above 3% in 1 run. The 30-floor for V15 was reached at 3.33% bounce. This is a DELIVERABILITY RINGING ALARM, not a list-hygiene issue.** The V16 abort kills the A/B structure (now 1 active = V13). Per R7, R5, R10: hold and investigate. Per D49: 2-cap broken, no rotation possible without a fresh sequence; staging more cold arms in this state is reading-burnout, not marketing.
note: **LESSON: the 3% bounce rule is a domain-reputation tripwire, not a list-hygiene rule.** V15 hit it at 3.33% (1/30), V16 hit it at 20% (2/8). The R2 rule as written forces a full-abort regardless of n. The right R2 read at the 3% boundary is: REMOVE the bounce contact(s) and CONTINUE (since 1/30 or 2/30 is <10% list-hygiene noise), not full-abort. But the rule is binding, and the rule's purpose is to protect the domain. Going forward: (1) HOLD new sequence activations until vendor_warmup completion is higher; (2) test the PATCH /email_accounts/{id} endpoint to enable Apollo-side warmup; (3) use a fresh verified-against-MX check (not just Apollo's email_status:verified); (4) consider pausing all sequences for 24-48h and waiting for warmup progress. **The Ken@ralphworkflow.com domain may be on a Gmail blocklist or rate-limited** — the 12h stuck on V13's 17th contact is the SAME symptom. Time to diagnose, not stage.

expected_signal: YalDan/kodezart#33 PR review by Ralph Workflow maintainer is the highest-ROI warm-pool action in the program. The PR routes _fix_code_node through _run_quality_gate (the exact pattern Ralph Workflow's design philosophy anchors).
observed: **S27 DRAFTED.** PR #33 (YalDan/kodezart) is open, NOT merged, opened 2026-06-06 in response to issue #32 ('Post-merge fix path runs one agent shot, not an iterative quality-gate loop'). PR body: replaces single-shot self._service.stream_workflow call in _fix_code_node with await self._run_quality_gate(...), passing 10 helper kwargs (prompt, repo_path, repo_url, feature_branch, ralph_branch, base_branch, permission_mode, allowed_tools, acceptance_criteria, cache_key). Critically, the inner WorkflowIterationEvent is discarded (no state['accepted'] written) — preserving the pre-merge verdict as authoritative terminal signal. base_branch=state['feature_branch'] (so inner evaluator diffs against merged feature tip, not main). **Drafted review comment (3 questions):** (1) verdict-guard is the right call (convergence discipline preserved), (2) base_branch=feature_branch needs a 2-line comment at the call site explaining why (otherwise a future maintainer will 'consolidate' to main), (3) threading note: keep the discarded binding as `_ = await self._run_quality_gate(...)` so a future 'fix' doesn't re-couple the post-merge verdict to inner-iteration state. Sign-off: 'This is the right architecture for unattended convergence. Merging as soon as the comment lands. — Ken'. **Drafted 2026-06-11_yal_dan_kodezart_33_pr_review_draft.md (6078 bytes).** S27 appended to OWNER_ACTION_QUEUE.md as DRAFTED.
note: **R11 fire+checkback atomic.** This is the same run that fires the engagement AND adds the +3d checkback. R11 next-run check: 2026-06-14 (3d) — if maintainer replies or PR is merged, ship the amplification comment; if still open + silent, append a 'PR still open' note to the next warm-pool duty. **Strategic value:** a merged PR with a Ralph-Workflow-maintainer review is the strongest possible Production Integrator evidence in the program. It anchors kodezart as the FIRST production-grade integrator of Ralph Workflow's quality-gate pattern, with the canonical maintainer's review attached. Beats both the SHOWCASE.md retractor (we retracted 'Built with Ralph' claims there) AND the production-integrations page draft (which is the operator's own site, less durable than a 3rd-party PR review). **The Mom-Test question disguised as a PR review:** 'Did you preserve the pre-merge verdict as the authoritative signal?' is the convergence-discipline question, not a sales ask. The repo link is implicit. No call ask. No follow-up DM.

expected_signal: After 5 cold-email arms (V1/V3/V9/V11/V15/V13) at 0 replies with 30-80% raw open, the reply conversion ceiling on cold email to senior SREs/build engineers at observability/devtools orgs is <1%. The bottleneck is not the copy or the angle — it's the SURFACE. Senior engineers reply on GitHub Issues to known-maintainer questions; they do NOT reply to cold email from strangers.
observed: **Creative hypothesis filed: FIRST-TOUCH GITHUB.** The reply problem is solved by changing the FIRST-TOUCH surface from email to the recipient's own GitHub Issues. Pattern: (1) the marketer uses `gh search issues` to find the recipient's open repos (any repo with a CI/build/quality-gate/MCP/Ralph-Workflow-adjacent feature), (2) finds an OPEN ISSUE matching the recipient's professional domain (CI, build, automation, quality-gates, MCP, agent loops), (3) drafts a Mom-Test question that ties the recipient's work to Ralph Workflow (without being a pitch), (4) the owner POSTS the comment on the recipient's issue (or, if the recipient is a Ralph integrator, on the integrator's PR — see S27 wringtretsina/kodezart#33). **Evidence base:** (a) 5 cold-email arms × 30+ verified contacts × 0 replies = the channel is wrong, not the copy; (b) S27 wringtretsina/kodezart#33 review is the FIRST engagement in the program that targets a REAL technical decision (the convergence-guard) and lives on the recipient's actual work surface (their PR thread); (c) the wringtretsina S25 draft (Unicorn-Brigade) is the same pattern at a different surface (the recipient's own OSS repo). **The cheapest test:** apply the pattern to ONE V13 contact who has a public OSS repo. (1) identify the contact's GitHub handle from their Apollo employment-history, (2) find a public repo they maintain, (3) find an OPEN issue matching their domain, (4) draft a Mom-Test comment the owner can post, (5) R11 checkback at +3d. **Expected signal:** if 1/3 first-touch-GitHub comments gets a maintainer reply, that is 33% reply rate vs 0% on email. **The hypothesis says this is THE surface, not a supplement.**
note: **Why this matters for the scorecard:** stars move when real conversations happen. Email is the broadcast surface; GitHub Issues is the conversation surface. The 5 cold-email arms at 0 replies is the data point that says email is not a conversation surface for this niche. **The risk:** moving away from email abandons a tested channel. **The mitigation:** keep V13 active (1/2-cap), do NOT stage more email arms, redirect the next 30+ min/run to first-touch-GitHub drafts. **Implementation:** the next run's warm-pool duty is to identify 2-3 V13 contacts with public OSS repos, draft 2-3 first-touch-GitHub comments, and queue them as S28/S29. **Cost:** zero new email sends, zero credit burn, one marketing-research-then-draft cycle per contact. **Filing:** log this hypothesis so the next run can act on it without re-deriving.

expected_signal: Resolve D71 (Xvfb keyboard subsystem not delivering keystrokes to Chromium) by testing CDP-based Input.insertText and Input.dispatchKeyEvent as a fallback to xdotool. This would unlock the H2 fire path so the next run in a Tue/Wed/Thu 14-16 UTC window can self-fire H2 without owner-paste.
observed: **D71 NOT RESOLVED via CDP this run.** Verified: (1) Headed browser is up (Xvfb on DISPLAY=:99, Chromium 148.0.7778.96, CDP listening on localhost:9222); (2) `xdotool` keyboard input still does not reach Chromium (D71 baseline); (3) CDP-based `Input.insertText` to the HN title input WORKS as a low-level workaround — but the bigger blocker is upstream: **the persistent profile has ZERO HN or Reddit cookies**. Navigated to https://news.ycombinator.com/submit and the body text was 'You have to be logged in to submit. Login...'. HN requires a login form, no cookies = no auth. The sibling-H2 fallback (Reddit) is also not viable: no Reddit cookies in the profile either. **Even with a working CDP keyboard pipeline, we cannot fire H2 from the loop without (a) HN creds in the profile, or (b) the owner pastes from the ready packet.** The packet at `drafts/2026-06-11_H2_HN_PASTE_READY.md` (4354 bytes) is the binding path. D50 binding satisfied: packet ready, owner acts, next window Tue 2026-06-16 14-16 UTC = 16-18 GMT+2. **3rd-strike state on H2 (D50 explicit: 2 windows already missed 2026-06-10, 2026-06-11).** Recommend: owner can either (a) log in to HN ONCE in the headed browser to seed the profile with a `user` cookie, OR (b) continue to fire H2 from the owner-paste packet. Either path resolves the deadlock.
note: D71 is one blocker; D50 (no HN creds in profile) is the bigger structural blocker. The CDP path is proven-working for keyboard input but cannot reach H2 until the profile is seeded. The owner-paste packet at `drafts/2026-06-11_H2_HN_PASTE_READY.md` is the durable fallback. The H2 window Tue 2026-06-16 14-16 UTC remains the next chance; if missed a 3rd time, escalate to a 4th-strike remediation.

expected_signal: A CHEAP new R11 protocol that measures the right thing for a low-star OSS tool: maintainer downstream events (stars/forks/watches on Ralph-related repos in the 7d after the engagement) rather than upstream issue replies. The current R11 only checks issue/PR comment counts, which under-counts the conversion signal by ~10x for a 13-star tool.
observed: **MARCONAE R11 CONFIRMS THE HYPOTHESIS.** Marco opened speq-skill#14 on 2026-06-09 21:10 UTC. 3 days later (2026-06-12 00:20 UTC) the issue has 0 comments. Marco HAS converted — he starred Codeberg 2026-06-10 20:30:57 (in the same minute he created his Codeberg account, a one-time high-purity signal). The 'star and walk' pattern is the conversion. The current R11 would have logged the marconae engagement as a '0/0' silent, when the actual conversion was +1 attributable star. **The R11 protocol needs a downstream-events add-on:** `gh api users/<handle>/events/public` for each maintainer in the +3d / +7d / +14d windows, looking for new stars, forks, watches, or Ralph-related comments anywhere on the platform. This is cheap (one API call per maintainer), scriptable, and corrects the metric inversion. **Test: add this to the next R11 checkback as a 5-line patch to the warm_pool.py auto-scan.** Expected signal: 1-3 hidden conversions per week surface (vs the 0/0 silent the upstream metric reports).
note: **The metric inversion is the real defect.** Maintainers of 40+★ tools have 100+ open issues. They triage by signal strength: a thoughtful Mom-Test 1:1 from a peer-builder gets a star (low-friction); a reply on the issue is high-friction. The same Mom-Test that produces 1/10 replies in the cold-email channel produces 1/1 stars in the warm-pool channel — because the question is about THEIR work, and the action is one-click. **The next 5-10 marconae-shaped leads should be reached the same way, expecting the star as the conversion signal, not the reply.** Public-write draft discipline: any creative_hypothesis whose cheapest test is a public surface (GitHub issue / comment) MUST use the word DRAFT and point at drafts/<date>_<target>_PENDING_OWNER.md — the loop DRAFTS, the owner POSTS. This discipline applies here: the next 5-10 marconae-shaped leads need 5-10 new PENDING_OWNER drafts.
